title: Benthamantha ochroleuca
intro: Benthamantha ochroleuca, also known as the yellow-green Benthamantha, is a species of flowering plant in the legume family Fabaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Benthamantha ochroleuca include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Benthamantha ochroleuca is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, respiratory infections, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Benthamantha ochroleuca is classified as a species within the genus Benthamantha and the family Fabaceae. The genus Benthamantha is a part of the subfamily Faboideae. The family Fabaceae is a part of the order Fabales. It is a flowering plant native to tropical Africa. Benthamantha ochroleuca is also known by its synonym, Wajira ochroleuca.
